{"signals":[{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"pet","confidence":0.97,"matched_text":"And congratulations on the new pet bed for Luna!","distilled_fact":"Luna has a new pet bed."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"pet","confidence":0.81,"matched_text":"I'm sure she's loving it.","distilled_fact":"Luna is loving her new pet bed."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"pet camera feature preference","confidence":0.94,"matched_text":"When it comes to pet cameras, there are many great options available. Here are some factors to consider:

1. **Video Quality**: Look for a camera with high-definition video (720p or 1080p) for clear footage.
2. **Field of View**: Consider a camera with a wide-angle lens (around 130-150°) to capture more of the room.
3. **Night Vision**: If you want to monitor your pets at night, look for a camera with infrared or night vision capabilities.
4. **Two-Way Audio**: A camera with two-way audio allows you to communicate with your pets remotely, which can be soothing for them.
5. **Motion Detection**: This feature alerts you to any movement in the room, so you can check in on your pets if you receive an alert.
6. **Cloud Storage**: Consider a camera with cloud storage options, so you can review footage from previous days.
7. **App Compatibility**: Make sure the camera is compatible with your smartphone or tablet operating system.","distilled_fact":"A preferred pet camera may have high-definition video, a wide-angle lens, night vision capabilities, two-way audio, motion detection, cloud storage options, and smartphone or tablet operating system compatibility."},{"signal_kind":"potential_claim","object_kind":"pet camera product","confidence":0.93,"matched_text":"1. **Furbo Dog Camera**: A highly-rated camera with 1080p video, night vision, and two-way audio.
